mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) directly expands corporate earnings capacity, and investors price this improvement into equity valuations within one month, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly growth and forward 20-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below the natural rate threshold of 5%, tightening labor market conditions stimulate wage growth and consumer spending, which directly benefits corporate profits and drives stock prices higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: In months when UNRATE < 5.0, the share of positive 20-day forward returns is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.10) over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% signals a deliberate tightening cycle that raises discount rates on equity cash flows and increases the hurdle rate for corporate investment, causing a near-term negative repricing of stocks within five trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.0, the proportion of positive 5-day forward returns exceeds 50% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that consumer demand is pulling through manufacturing, creating a virtuous cycle of rising revenues and output that sustains corporate earnings and drives equity prices higher over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS yoy growth > 5% and INDPRO yoy growth > 5%
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling period, forward 60-day returns following joint activation are not significantly positive (Sharpe ratio < 0.5).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll growth decelerating by more than 100,000 jobs month-over-month signals an abrupt loss of labor market momentum, which precedes weakening consumer income and spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS MoM decline exceeds 100k, the median 20-day forward return is above zero over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in real retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% indicates a sudden consumer retrenchment that immediately lowers earnings expectations and triggers a defensive rotation out of equities within five trading days.
failure_condition: In months with RSAFS MoM decline >2%, the proportion of positive 5-day forward returns is above 50% over any 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 4%, real interest rates turn deeply negative, forcing investors out of cash and bonds into equities as the only return-generating asset, lifting stock prices over 60 days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns in activation periods are not significantly higher than in non-activation periods (difference in means t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment gains above 200,000 per month concurrent with an unemployment rate below 5.5% signals a structurally strong labor market that supports durable consumer expenditure and corporate pricing power, producing positive equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: In activation months, the hit rate of positive 20-day forward returns falls below 55% over any 5-year rolling window.
